summary: Fixed photo import/upload display issues in Art Connect Africa. The root cause was that uploaded files at /uploads/ were being intercepted by the frontend routing instead of being served by the backend. Fixed by mounting uploads at /api/uploads/ in backend and updating getMediaUrl() to convert paths accordingly.
passed_tests: Avatar upload endpoint exists and works (POST /api/artists/me/avatar)
Avatar upload rejects invalid file types
Avatar upload updates user profile correctly
Uploaded avatar files are accessible via /api/uploads/
Feed posts display images correctly (no broken images)
External URLs (unsplash, dicebear) are preserved unchanged
Local uploads have correct /uploads/ path in database
Settings page shows avatar upload button
Discover page artist cards show avatars correctly
Feed page shows post images and author avatars
Artist profile page displays avatar correctly
Create post dialog has image upload option
context_for_next_testing_agent: Image upload and display is fully working. The key fix was routing: uploads are now served at /api/uploads/ instead of /uploads/ to ensure proper Kubernetes ingress routing to the backend. The getMediaUrl() utility in frontend converts paths automatically. All components (Dashboard, Settings, Feed, ArtistCard, ArtistProfile) use getMediaUrl() for proper image URL resolution.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The Kubernetes ingress routes /api/* to backend and all other paths to frontend. Uploaded files were stored at /uploads/ which was being intercepted by the frontend React router instead of being served by the backend's StaticFiles mount.
reproduction_steps: 3. Frontend tries to load image from https://domain/uploads/avatars/{id}.png
4. Ingress routes this to frontend (not /api prefix)
5. Frontend returns React app HTML instead of image
fix_applied: 1. Changed backend to mount uploads at /api/uploads instead of /uploads
2. Updated getMediaUrl() to convert /uploads/ paths to /api/uploads/
3. Now images load correctly via /api/uploads/ which routes to backend
